course: CMU 21-254: Linear Algebra and Vector Calculus for Engineers
audience: SI leader teaching live on a whiteboard. This is not a student worksheet.
toneRule: Keep explanations very simple, verbal, and board-friendly. Avoid textbook phrasing unless the problem needs it.
title: Curves, Tangent Vectors, and Arc Length
sessionGoal: Students should be able to read a parametrized curve, find velocity/speed/unit tangent, parametrize a line segment, and compute simple arc length.
quickStartIfUnprepared: Start with the sentence: a parametrized curve is a moving point.
Do Problem 1 to review position, velocity, speed, and unit tangent.
Do Problem 2 if students struggle with parametrizing segments.
Do Problem 3 to show a clean arc length example.
sayThisFirst: A curve \(r(t)\) is a position that changes with time.
\(r'(t)\) is the velocity vector, so it points tangent to the curve.
Speed is the length of velocity: \(\|r'(t)\|\).
Arc length adds up speed over the interval.
boardPlan: Write position, velocity, speed, unit tangent.
Show line segment parametrization from A to B.
Finish with one arc length integral where speed is constant.
keyFormulas: Line segment from A to B: \(r(t)=A+t(B-A),\ 0\le t\le1\).
commonMistakes: Forgetting that velocity is a vector but speed is a number.
Using \(A-B\) instead of \(B-A\) for a segment from A to B.
Forgetting the interval for a parametrization.
Integrating \(r(t)\) instead of \(\|r'(t)\|\) for arc length.
notationWarnings: Use \(\overrightarrow{AB}=B-A\) for the vector from A to B.
The unit tangent only exists when \(r'(t)\ne 0\).

problems.title: Position, velocity, speed, and unit tangent
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(r(t)=(t^2,t^3-3t,2t)\). Find \(r(1)\), \(r'(1)\), the speed at \(t=1\), and the unit tangent vector at \(t=1\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: take the derivative component-by-component. Then speed is just the length of that derivative vector.

drawInstructions: Draw a curve in 3D or as a simple squiggle.
Draw an arrow tangent to the curve labeled r'(1).
Label speed as the length of r'(1), not a new direction.

walkthrough.label: Divide by speed to get the unit tangent

commonMistakes: Calling \(r'(1)\) the speed. \(r'(1)\) is velocity; its magnitude is speed.
Forgetting to divide by the speed for the unit tangent.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 7 for curves and tangent vectors.

problems.title: Parametrize a line segment
problems.studentPromptLatex: Parametrize the line segment from \(A=(1,2,0)\) to \(B=(4,-1,3)\). Then find its length.
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: start at A, then add t times the vector from A to B.

drawInstructions: Draw point A and point B.
Draw an arrow from A to B.
Show t=0 gives A and t=1 gives B.

walkthrough.label: Find the length
walkthrough.latex: The length of the segment is \[\|\overrightarrow{AB}\|=\sqrt{3^2+(-3)^2+3^2}=\sqrt{27}=3\sqrt3.\]

commonMistakes: Writing a line instead of a line segment.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 7 for parametrized curves.

problems.title: Arc length of a helix
problems.studentPromptLatex: Find the arc length of \(r(t)=(3\cos t,3\sin t,4t)\) for \(0\le t\le2\pi\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: arc length is not about the position vector. It is the integral of speed.

drawInstructions: Draw a helix wrapping upward around the z-axis.
Draw a tangent arrow and label its length as 5.
Explain that constant speed makes the integral easy.

commonMistakes: Integrating each component separately instead of speed.
Dropping the vertical component \(4t\), which changes the length.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 7 for arc length.

wrapUpQuestions: What is the difference between velocity and speed?
How do you parametrize from A to B?
qualityControlNotes: Checked r(1), velocity, speed, and unit tangent.
Checked line segment direction and length.
Checked helix speed is constant 5 and length is 10pi.
reviewSummary: Session 16 is ready with exact visual specs for curve tangent, segment, and helix sketches.
